betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Blowtorch & Acetylene Welding Torch§f

§aPortable welding tools for assembling advanced machinery and structures!§f

Two tiers of welding torches, both used for welding cast steel plates to blocks.

§6Blowtorch§f:
§b• Fuel§f: §e4,000 mB§f of Natural Gas (250 mB per operation)
§b• Recipe§f: Copper Plate ×3 + Iron Ingot

§6Acetylene Welding Torch§f:
§b• Fuel§f: §e8,000 mB§f Unsaturated Hydrocarbons + §e16,000 mB§f Liquid Oxygen (10 mB + 20 mB per operation)
§b• Recipe§f: Steel Plate ×3 + Bakelite Bar + Steel Tank
§b• Note§f: This is the §eupgraded§f version with much larger fuel capacity and longer operation time per fuel load.

§6Common Uses§f:
§b• Welding cast steel plates§f to various blocks
§b• Assembling §emultiblock structures§f that require welded joints

§eTip§f: Start with the basic Blowtorch (Natural Gas is easy to produce). Upgrade to the Acetylene Welding Torch when you have unsaturated hydrocarbons and liquid oxygen production set up — it lasts much longer between refuels!
